root_text.sn46.56:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.sn46.56:1.3: Atha kho abhayo rājakumāro yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho abhayo rājakumāro bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn46.56:1.7: Natthi hetu, natthi paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya.
root_text.sn46.56:1.10: “Atthi, rājakumāra, hetu, atthi paccayo aññāṇāya adassanāya.
root_text.sn46.56:1.12: Atthi, rājakumāra, hetu, atthi paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya.
root_text.sn46.56:2.1: “Katamo pana, bhante, hetu, katamo paccayo aññāṇāya adassanāya?
root_text.sn46.56:2.2: Kathaṁ sahetu, sappaccayo aññāṇaṁ adassanaṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn46.56:2.3: “Yasmiṁ kho, rājakumāra, samaye kāmarāgapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati kāmarāgaparetena, uppannassa ca kāmarāgassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati—
root_text.sn46.56:2.4: ayampi kho, rājakumāra, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo aññāṇāya adassanāya.
root_text.sn46.56:2.5: Evampi sahetu sappaccayo aññāṇaṁ adassanaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.56:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, rājakumāra, yasmiṁ samaye byāpādapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati byāpādaparetena …pe…
root_text.sn46.56:3.4: vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati vicikicchāparetena, uppannāya ca vicikicchāya nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati—
root_text.sn46.56:3.5: ayampi kho, rājakumāra, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo aññāṇāya adassanāya.
root_text.sn46.56:3.6: Evampi sahetu sappaccayo aññāṇaṁ adassanaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.sn46.56:4.5: Ekamekenapi kho, bhante, nīvaraṇena abhibhūto yathābhūtaṁ na jāneyya na passeyya, ko pana vādo pañcahi nīvaraṇehi?
root_text.sn46.56:5.1: Katamo pana, bhante, hetu, katamo paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya?
root_text.sn46.56:5.2: Kathaṁ sahetu, sappaccayo ñāṇaṁ dassanaṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn46.56:5.3: “Idha, rājakumāra, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.56:5.4: So satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāvitena cittena yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati—
root_text.sn46.56:5.5: ayampi kho, rājakumāra, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya.
root_text.sn46.56:5.6: Evampi sahetu, sappaccayo ñāṇaṁ dassanaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.56:6.2: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.56:6.3: So upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāvitena cittena yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati—
root_text.sn46.56:6.4: ayampi kho, rājakumāra, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya.
root_text.sn46.56:6.5: Evaṁ sahetu, sappaccayo ñāṇaṁ dassanaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.sn46.56:7.5: Ekamekenapi kho, bhante, bojjhaṅgena samannāgato yathābhūtaṁ jāneyya passeyya, ko pana vādo sattahi bojjhaṅgehi?
root_text.sn46.56:7.6: Yopi me, bhante, gijjhakūṭaṁ pabbataṁ ārohantassa kāyakilamatho cittakilamatho, sopi me paṭippassaddho, dhammo ca me abhisamito”ti.

translation_text.sn46.56:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.sn46.56:1.3: Then Prince Abhaya went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn46.56:1.5: ‘There is no cause or reason for not knowing and not seeing.
translation_text.sn46.56:1.6: Not knowing and not seeing have no cause or reason.
translation_text.sn46.56:1.7: There is no cause or reason for knowing and seeing.
translation_text.sn46.56:1.8: Knowing and seeing have no cause or reason.’
translation_text.sn46.56:1.9: What does the Buddha say about this?”
translation_text.sn46.56:1.10: “Prince, there are causes and reasons for not knowing and not seeing.
translation_text.sn46.56:1.11: Not knowing and not seeing have causes and reasons.
translation_text.sn46.56:1.12: There are causes and reasons for knowing and seeing.
translation_text.sn46.56:1.13: Knowing and seeing have causes and reasons.”
translation_text.sn46.56:2.1: “But sir, what is the cause and reason for not knowing and not seeing?
translation_text.sn46.56:2.2: How do not knowing and not seeing have causes and reasons?”
translation_text.sn46.56:2.3: “There’s a time when the heart is overcome and mired in sensual desire, without truly knowing and seeing the escape from sensual desire that has arisen.
translation_text.sn46.56:2.4: This is a cause and reason for not knowing and not seeing.
translation_text.sn46.56:2.5: And this is how not knowing and not seeing have causes and reasons.
translation_text.sn46.56:3.1: Furthermore, there’s a time when the heart is overcome and mired in ill will …
translation_text.sn46.56:3.4: doubt, without truly knowing and seeing the escape from doubt that has arisen.
translation_text.sn46.56:3.5: This is a cause and reason for not knowing and not seeing.
translation_text.sn46.56:3.6: And this is how not knowing and not seeing have causes and reasons.”
translation_text.sn46.56:4.1: “Sir, what is the name of this exposition of the teaching?”
translation_text.sn46.56:4.4: Indeed, Holy One, these are hindrances!
translation_text.sn46.56:4.5: Overcome by even a single hindrance you wouldn’t truly know or see, let alone all five hindrances.
translation_text.sn46.56:5.1: But sir, what is the cause and reason for knowing and seeing?
translation_text.sn46.56:5.2: How do knowing and seeing have causes and reasons?”
translation_text.sn46.56:5.3: “It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factor of mindfulness, which relies on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripens as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.56:5.4: They truly know and see with a mind that has developed the awakening factor of mindfulness.
translation_text.sn46.56:5.5: This is a cause and reason for knowing and seeing.
translation_text.sn46.56:5.6: And this is how knowing and seeing have causes and reasons.
translation_text.sn46.56:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant develops the awakening factor of investigation of principles … energy … rapture … tranquility … immersion …
translation_text.sn46.56:6.2: equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.56:6.3: They truly know and see with a mind that has developed the awakening factor of equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.56:6.4: This is a cause and reason for knowing and seeing.
translation_text.sn46.56:6.5: And this is how knowing and seeing have causes and reasons.”
translation_text.sn46.56:7.1: “Sir, what is the name of this exposition of the teaching?”
translation_text.sn46.56:7.3: “Indeed, Blessed One, these are awakening factors!
translation_text.sn46.56:7.4: Indeed, Holy One, these are awakening factors!
translation_text.sn46.56:7.5: Endowed with even a single awakening factor you would truly know and see, let alone all seven awakening factors.
translation_text.sn46.56:7.6: When climbing Mount Vulture’s Peak I became fatigued in body and mind. But this has now faded away. And I’ve comprehended the teaching.”
